ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT SCOTT TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT SCOTT TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED AND ITS ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO ISSUES 1 THROUGH 4 AND OFF CALENDAR AS TO ISSUE 5. MR. ROMAN'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY AND DECLARATION CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER HE WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS-CONTAINING PRODUCTS OR MATERIALS ATTRIBUTABLE TO DEFENDANT. DR. COHEN'S DECLARATION CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER DEFENDANT OWED A DUTY TO MR. ROMAN - WHETHER DEFENDANT KNEW OR SHOULD HAVE KNOWN OF THE HAZARDS OF ASBESTOS EXPOSURE. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO DR. COHEN'S DECLARATION ARE OVERRULED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO SUSTAIN ITS BURDEN ON THE ISSUE OF WHETHER DEFENDANT KNEW OR SHOULD HAVE KNOWN WHETHER THE MATERIALS ATTRIBUTABLE TO DEFENDANT TO WHICH MR. ROMAN WAS ALLEGEDLY EXPOSED CONTAINED ASBESTOS. PLAINTIFFS HAVE DISMISSED THEIR CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, REPORTER: PATRICIA RODRIGUEZ, CSR #12436

DEFENDANT RPI COMPANY'S MOTION TO VACATE TRIAL DATE AND REOPEN DISCOVERY

No objection made and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant RPI Company's motion to vacate trial date and reopen discovery: The court treated defendant's motion as a motion to continue the trial date, which is granted. The trial date, which has already passed and which has a current return date of May 13, 2013, is continued to March 17, 2014 at 11:15am in Department 503. MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kristina F. Almquist of MORGAN LEWIS (415-442-1227), appearing for defendant / moving party Scott Technologies, Inc.
Jennifer L. Alesio of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Anthony Roman Sr., et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT SCOTT TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Scott Technologies, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 through 4 and off calendar as to issue 5. Mr. Roman's deposition testimony and declaration create a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Dr. Cohen's declaration creates a triable issue whether defendant owed a duty to Mr. Roman - whether defendant knew or should have known of the hazards of asbestos exposure. Defendant's objections to Dr. Cohen's declaration are overruled. Defendant failed to sustain its burden on the issue of whether defendant knew or should have known whether the materials attributable to defendant to which Mr. Roman was allegedly exposed contained asbestos. Plaintiffs have dismissed their claim for punitive damages. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez, CSR #12436 (503/TLJ)
